title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Volunteering is a (0) REWARDING (REWARD) experience that benefits both the individual and the community. Engaging in (17) CHARITABLE (CHARITY) work allows people to develop new skills while supporting a good cause. Many find that the (18) SATISFACTION (SATISFY) gained from helping others is (19) INCOMPARABLE (COMPARE) to any monetary gain. Furthermore, volunteering can (20) STRENGTHEN (STRONG) social bonds and reduce feelings of (21) LONELINESS (ALONE) among participants. It provides an (22) OPPORTUNITY (OPPORTUNE) to meet people from diverse backgrounds, fostering a sense of (23) BELONGING (BELONG). (24) ADMITTEDLY (ADMIT), it requires a significant time commitment, but the personal growth achieved is invaluable. By contributing to the well-being of others, volunteers gain a broader perspective on life and a deeper understanding of the challenges faced by their neighbors, ultimately creating a more compassionate and cohesive society where everyone has the chance to thrive and feel valued for their unique contributions.

example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'experience'; REWARD → REWARDING.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'work'; CHARITY → CHARITABLE.
Noun required after the definite article; SATISFY → SATISFACTION.
Adjective with a negative prefix required; COMPARE → INCOMPARABLE.
Verb required after the modal 'can'; STRONG → STRENGTHEN.
Noun required after the indefinite article; OPPORTUNE → OPPORTUNITY.
Noun required after 'sense of'; BELONG → BELONGING.
Adverb required to introduce a concessive clause; ADMIT → ADMITTEDLY.
